title: Experimental Techniques in Nuclear and Particle Physics

cards.topic: Ch.1: Basic Nuclear Processes in Radioactive Sources
cards.q: What are $\alpha$ particles and what is an alpha decay
cards.a: Alpha particles are $^4$He nuclei, i.e., a bound system of two protons and two neutrons, and are generally emitted by very heavy nuclei containing too many nucleons to remain stable. $$(Z,A) \to (Z-2, A-4) + \alpha$$

cards.topic: Ch.1: Basic Nuclear Processes in Radioactive Sources
cards.q: Why $\alpha$ particles are usually emitted and not single nucleons?
cards.a: The emission of such a nucleon cluster as a whole rather than the emission of single nucleons is energetically more advantageous because of the particularly high binding energy of the $\alpha$-particle.
